content: In Estonia, while you are still married under a joint property regime, you cannot unilaterally sell "your half" of the marital assets or force a division of the joint property. According to Article 26(1) of the Estonian divorce laws, a spouse does not have the right to request the division of joint property while still married. Additionally, you cannot dispose of your share in the joint property without the consent of your spouse. Therefore, any division of assets must occur after the termination of the joint property regime, typically through legal proceedings or mutual agreement.

in this legal case in estonia the harju county court made a final decision after a 3year and 11month process the court ruled that one party is responsible for covering the procedural costs although the specific amount was not mentioned the court partially dismissed one partys claim and partially satisfied the other partys counterclaim the court divided the joint assets between the parties leaving certain shares and awarding compensation to each party the court rejected certain claims for the distribution of compensation and expenses incurred by the joint assets the court also stated that the procedural costs would be borne by the parties the court canceled a previous order and prohibited the disposal of certain property both parties have the option to appeal the courts decision within 30 days the court based its decision on the family law act and the property act the court considered whether certain assets should be recognized as joint or separate and whether certain expenses should be included in the joint assets the court also examined transfers made by one party to the other during the marriage to determine if they should be considered contributions to the joint assets the court found evidence to support some claims but dismissed others the court determined the value of certain assets based on the evidence provided the court also addressed the division of joint assets and ordered compensation to be paid to one party procedural costs were assigned to one party but the court partially exempted them from paying certain costs state legal aid was assigned to one party and the court ruled on the payment of procedural costs the summary does not provide specific details about the parties involved or the code articles referenced in the original text

ownership should be terminated and that the defendant should compensate the plaintiff for their share of the money used for the joint property the court will make a decision based on the evidence in this legal case a marriage between two parties was concluded in 2011 and they were divorced in 2022 they have joint assets including a property that is mortgaged the claimant wants to divide the property either through a public auction or by transferring it to the defendant with compensation the defendant agrees to sell the property through a public auction but also wants to acquire it through a counterclaim the court determines that the joint ownership should be terminated and the property should be sold through a public auction the parties are advised to reach a compromise the court also determines that the defendant should bear the procedural costs the claimant is awarded legal aid costs for the duration of the court hearing which were considered reasonable the total time spent on the hearing was 7 hours and 45 minutes additionally the defendant is ordered to compensate the claimants legal fees in the amount of 50 euros the judgment was partially annulled by the tallinn district court in 2023 a decision was made in a legal case involving two parties but the specific details are not provided

in this legal case a court in viru county issued a final resolution on 30012023 the case involved two parties nk and ak who were seeking a divorce the court decided to divide their joint assets as follows nk will keep a car worth 2500 euros and ak will compensate nk with 1250 euros nk will also be recognized as the sole owner of an apartment ak is required to change the owners entry in the land register to reflect nk as the sole owner the costs of the proceedings will be shared by both parties ak has the option to appeal the courts decision within 30 days the court also mentioned previous cases related to the division of joint assets and the determination of separate assets the court ruled that the apartment in question should be considered joint assets and divided according to the family law act the court also ordered ak to provide a statement of intent to change the owners entry in the land register the court determined that the procedural costs of the case should be borne by both parties

in this legal case the court issued a final resolution stating that one party is responsible for covering the procedural costs the court also ruled on the division of assets between the parties involved the defendant will have sole ownership of a garage and the furnishings appliances and electronics in the apartment they will also receive compensation from the common property the court allows for the right to appeal the decision within 30 days the claim made by one party is the basis for the dissolution of the marriage the court must determine the value of the joint assets and divide them accordingly the court also considers the loan obligations and payments made by the parties the court determines that the procedural costs should be borne by the parties involved

in a court case one party was ordered to cover the procedural costs the case involved the division of joint property between two parties the court decided to terminate one partys ownership of immovable property and transfer it to the other party the court also dismissed a claim for additional compensation and ruled on the division of furniture and appliances the court addressed other claims related to loan agreements property rights and lease agreements the case was initiated by one party filing a lawsuit following the dissolution of their marriage the court made its decision based on applicable laws and the circumstances of the case the court determined the division of joint property and the obligations of each party the court also considered the marital property agreement and relevant provisions of family law the court explained that joint property must be determined at the end of a marriage and that the applicable law for dividing joint property is based on the law in force at the time of acquiring the property the court also discussed the progress of a legal case involving apartment ownership and the dispute over the composition of joint property an expert assessment determined the market value of the properties involved the court made decisions regarding compensation for joint property and the division of obligations the court also addressed the ownership of furniture and equipment determining that some items should be considered joint property and others should remain in the sole ownership of one party the court rejected a claim for additional compensation and discussed the division of property rights and obligations arising from a lease agreement the court considered evidence presented by both parties and made its judgment based on the circumstances of the case the court also determined the distribution of procedural costs in the case

this legal text summarizes the progress of a civil case between two parties referred to as xx and cc in the pärnu county court in estonia xx requested the division of joint assets and compensation from cc following their divorce the court partially satisfied the claim and ordered the termination of joint ownership of a property leaving it solely in ccs ownership the court also ordered cc to compensate xx with €78083 the court considered the arguments and evidence presented by both parties and made its decision based on the current family law act the court deviated from the equal division of assets based on xxs lack of valid reasons for not contributing to the joint assets the court ordered the division of the assets and awarded compensation to xx the court also ruled that the loan balance should be deducted from the value of the immovable property the court found that the defendant should repay the loan taken out during their cohabitation the court ruled in favor of the defendant and ordered the claimant to compensate the defendant the court also found that the claimant used the loan amounts for personal expenses and should repay the loan taken to purchase joint assets the court determined the compensation amount to be €780825 the court released the claimant from the loan obligation the court agreed that the defendant had the right to set off the amount transferred to the claimants account the court found that the defendant no longer had the right to demand compensation from the claimant for loan payments the court allowed the claimants request to change the ownership record the court considered the claimants request to sell the property at a public auction and divide the money between the parties the court found that dividing the joint assets or selling them at auction may not be fair the court concluded that the costs of the proceedings should be borne by the parties involved

this legal text summarizes the progress of a court case in ljubljana regarding the division of assets between two exspouses the case started on july 9 2018 and ended on december 4 2021 lasting for a total of 3 years 4 months and 25 days the total value of the assets involved is estimated to be 100000 eur including real estate properties a garage business premises and funds in a bank account the division of these assets was determined as 45 for the plaintiff and 55 for the defendant the court considered the principles of the marriage family relations act and certain sections of the civil procedure code in making its decision the court ordered the defendant to provide documents authorizing the registration of the plaintiffs share in the immovable property garage and land the defendant was also required to pay the plaintiff a sum of eur 471354 each party is responsible for bearing their own costs the court considered the income and contributions of each spouse in determining the division of the coowned property the court partially upheld the applicants claim and ordered the division of the property and the registration of their share the court also made a judgment regarding the payment of money the court allowed the addressing of property issues during ongoing litigation under specific circumstances the court determined the shares of the coowned property to be 45 and 55 for the parties involved the court considered the costs of the proceedings based on the civil procedure act the judgment was mostly in favor of one party but had some unresolved aspects the resolution of the dispute was seen as fair and took into account the interests of both parties the text also provides instructions for lodging an appeal including the time limit required information and payment of court fees the document was dated december 14 2021 in ljubljana

37. Division (1) After termination of jointness of property the spouses shall divide joint property between themselves. A spouse may submit an application for the division of joint property to court already together with an action for divorce or annulment of marriage or together with an action for termination of jointness of property. (11) The composition of joint property shall be determined as of the termination of the proprietary relationship. (2) Until the division of joint property, the requirements for administration of joint property established in Subdivision 2 of this Subchapter shall apply to the possession, use and disposal of the joint property. (3) Joint property shall be divided between the spouses into equal parts pursuant to the provisions concerning the termination of common ownership unless otherwise agreed.

71 DIVISION OF CO-OWNED PROPERTY (1) Co-owned property of spouses shall be divided if their marriage is terminated. During marriage, co-owned property may be divided by agreement or on the proposal of either of the spouses. (2) The agreement referred to in the preceding paragraph shall also contain an agreement between the spouses on the amount of their co-owned property. (3) If the marital property agreement whereby the spouses modify the legal property regime does not define the manner of division of co-owned property, such co-owned property shall be divided according to the legal property regime rules, unless the spouses agree otherwise. Division shall be carried out according to the status of property as at the date of effect of the marital property agreement. FAMILY

24. Selection of proprietary relationship Prospective spouses may, by agreement, select a proprietary relationship from among the types of proprietary relations set out in Subchapter 2 of this Chapter before the contraction of marriage by an application for marriage pursuant to the procedure prescribed in the Vital Statistics Registration Act. The abovementioned declaration of intention takes effect upon the contraction of marriage. If the prospective spouses do not select a proprietary relationship by an application for marriage and do not enter into a marital property contract, the provisions regarding jointness of property shall apply to their proprietary relations as of the contraction of marriage. The provisions regarding jointness of property shall also apply if the spouses have not selected the type of proprietary relations by an agreement to apply Estonian law to the proprietary rights. If spouses select a proprietary relationship by an application for marriage and, in addition, enter into a marital property contract, the marital property contract shall be applied to the proprietary relations between the spouses.

26. Principle of unity of joint property A spouse cannot dispose of his or her share in joint property or in a single object included in joint property. A spouse does not have the right to request the division of joint property during the jointness of property. A claim owned by spouses jointly may be set off only against a claim the satisfaction of which out of the joint property is prescribed by law or by an agreement.

211. Marital property right (1) In the case of marriages contracted before entry into force of this Act, the provisions of this Act regarding jointness of property apply to the proprietary relations between spouses as of entry into force of this Act unless otherwise provided for in a marital property contract entered into before entry into force of this Act. (2) In the proprietary relationship of jointness of property the procedure for administration of separate property provided for in § 271 of this Act applies to the property acquired after entry into force of § 271
ARTICLE 215 SEPARATION OF PROPERTY The spouses may agree that each of them shall retain sole ownership of the property acquired during the marriage.

75 MANNER OF DIVIDING PROPERTY (1) After establishing the shares of co-owned property, spouses may agree on the manner of dividing the property. Their agreement to become co-owners of items in proportion to their shares of co-owned property shall also be considered division of property. (2) If there is no agreement on division of property, a court shall divide the property in accordance with the rules applying to the division of co-owned property. FAMILY

28. Principles of joint administration of joint property (1) Unless otherwise agreed, spouses shall jointly exercise the rights and perform the obligations relating to joint property. Spouses have the right to possess jointly the objects forming part of their joint property. (2) Spouses may transfer the right to administer joint property to one of the spouses by a marital property contract. (3) Spouses shall administer joint property in the interests of matrimonial cohabitation and regular management of the property. They shall inform each other of the acts of administration of property and of the financial status. (4) A spouse may require conclusion of transactions necessary for correction of an entry concerning an object included in the joint property to be entered in the register.

25 Joint property In the case of jointness of property, the objects and other proprietary rights of the spouses acquired during the jointness of property shall transfer into the joint ownership of the spouses (hereinafter joint property).

64. Divorce granted by vital statistics office A vital statistics office, except for a notary, may grant divorce upon agreement of the spouses on the basis of a joint written petition of the spouses if: 1) the spouses have entered into, on the basis of Council Regulation (EU) No 1259/2010 implementing enhanced cooperation in the area of the law applicable to divorce and legal separation (OJ L 343, 29.12.2010, p. 10-16), an agreement on applicable law under which Estonian law applies to the divorce, or 2) both spouses reside in Estonia and Estonian law applies to the divorce.

ARTICLE 178 ASSETS INTENDED FOR THE OPERATION OF BUSINESS Assets intended for the operation of the enterprise of one of the spouses set up after the marriage and the assets of the enterprise set up even earlier are considered to be goods of community only if they exist at the time of its dissolution.

58. Proprietary relations between spouses in case of separateness of property In case of separateness of property, in proprietary relations spouses shall be deemed to be persons not married to each other. This does not affect application of the provisions of Chapters 3 and 5 of this Act.

68 SPOUSES' SHARES IN CO-OWNED PROPERTY (1) Co-owned property of spouses shall belong jointly to both spouses. Shares belonging to each spouse shall not be specified in the co-owned property. (2) A spouse may not dispose of their unspecified share of co-owned property by legal transactions inter vivos, and in particular, they may not expropriate or encumber it. (3) Items that are part of co-owned property shall be jointly owned by the spouses. FAMILY

ARTICLE 179 PERSONAL PROPERTY They do not constitute objects of community and are personal property of the spouse: (a) property owned by the spouse before the marriage or had a right in rem to enjoy; (b) property acquired after marriage by way of gift or inheritance, where it is not specified in the gift deed or will that it is to be allocated to the community; (c) property for the strictly personal use of each spouse and its accessories; (d) property used in the exercise of the spouse's profession, except property used for the purpose of running a business forming part of the community; (e) property obtained by way of damages as well as the pension relating to the partial or total loss of the capacity to work; (f) goods acquired with the price of the transfer of the personal property listed above or by exchange thereof, provided that this is expressly stated at the time of purchase. The acquisition of real property, or of movable property listed in Article 2683, made after the marriage, shall be excluded from community of property, within the meaning of points (c), (d) and (f) of the preceding paragraph, where such exclusion is apparent from the deed of acquisition if the other spouse was also a party to it.

36. Action for termination of jointness of property (1) A spouse may file an action for termination of jointness of property if: 1) his or her rights are significantly endangered since the other spouse has repeatedly abused his or her right to administer the property or since the other spouse does not participate in regular management of joint property without adequate reason; 2) the other spouse has violated the obligation to maintain the family and this may endanger significantly maintenance of the family. (2) If jointness of property is terminated on the basis of subsection 1 of this section, the court shall send a copy of the corresponding court decision to the Chamber of Notaries for making the corresponding entry in the marital property register. (3) Separateness of property arises between spouses upon termination of the proprietary relationship of jointness of property on the basis of this section.

ARTICLE 159 STATUTORY MATRIMONIAL PROPERTY REGIME The statutory matrimonial property regime (in the absence of a different agreement concluded pursuant to Article 162, according to which marital agreements must be concluded by authentic instrument under penalty of nullity, the choice of the separation regime may also be declared in the marriage ceremony, the agreements may be concluded at any time) shall be community of property governed by artt. 177 ff.

57. Creation of separateness of property (1) Upon marriage spouses may select separateness of property as a proprietary relationship pursuant to the procedure provided for in the Vital Statistics Registration Act or prescribe separateness of property as a proprietary relationship by a marital property contract. (2) Separateness of property arises also upon the termination of jointness of property based on a marital property contract or § 36 of this Act unless another type of proprietary relationship is prescribed by the marital property contract. The same applies if the proprietary relationship of set-off of assets increment is terminated based on a marital property contract or § 45 of this Act.

27. Separate property Joint property does not include the separate property of neither of the spouses. The separate property of a spouse includes: 1) personal effects of the spouse; 2) objects which were in the ownership of the spouse before the marriage or objects acquired by the spouse during the marriage by disposal without charge, including as a gift or by succession; 3) objects which the spouse acquires on the basis of a right belonging to his or her separate property or as compensation for the destruction of, damage to or seizure of objects included in his or her separate property or on the basis of a transaction entered into with regard to his or her separate property; 4) units of a mandatory pension fund, money in the pension investment account and financial assets acquired for the money, including the proprietary rights arising from the contract entered into upon acquisition of such financial assets, and the proprietary rights arising from the insurance contract for a mandatory funded pension. The expenses incurred by both spouses in the form of work and proprietary performance during jointness of property in order to receive benefit from the property (necessary and useful expenses) are not included in separate property. The value of such expenses shall be included in the joint property of the spouses. Single objects or certain type of objects may be declared to be joint property or separate property by a marital property contract. An object of property shall be deemed to be included in the joint property of the spouses until the inclusion thereof in the separate property of a spouse is proved. Inclusion of an object of property in the separate property of a spouse shall apply with regard to third persons only in accordance with the provisions of § 61 of this Act.

69 JOINT MANAGEMENT AND USE (1) The co-owned property of spouses shall be managed and disposed of jointly and by agreement. (2) If one of the spouses uses movable property of small value or if such spouse manages the co-owned property it shall be deemed that they have the consent of the other spouse. FAMILY

64.1 Divorce granted by notary and entering into agreement on law applicable to divorce (1) A notary may grant divorce upon agreement of the spouses on the basis of a joint written petition of the spouses if Estonia has jurisdiction in this divorce matter in accordance with Council Regulation (EU) 2019/1111 on jurisdiction, the recognition and enforcement of decisions in matrimonial matters and the matters of parental responsibility, and on international child abduction (recast) (OJ L 178, 2.7.2019, p. 1-115). (2) The spouses may enter into an agreement on the law applicable to the divorce corresponding to Council Regulation (EU) No 1259/2010 in person in a notarially authenticated form. (3) The entering into an agreement on the law applicable to the divorce in judicial proceedings shall be recorded. The recording shall substitute for the notarially authenticated form. (4) The spouses may enter into and amend the agreements specified in subsections 2 and 3 of this section at any time until a petition for divorce is accepted by a notary or in judicial proceedings until the time specified in subsection 2 of § 2 of the Private International Law Act.

content: To change your matrimonial property regime in Italy, the agreement must be formalized in the form of a public deed, which means it needs to be executed before a notary. This requirement is outlined in Article 210 of the Italian Civil Code. The agreement will bind third parties only after it has been registered in the appropriate public registers. Therefore, it is essential to ensure that the registration is completed to provide legal effect against third parties.
contexts: this legal text describes a court ruling in italy regarding a dispute between two parties over the division of their shared property the court has decided to dissolve the shared property and has allocated specific buildings and lands to each party based on their respective shares one party has been ordered to compensate the other for the loss of enjoyment of the property the court has also deemed certain requests for the cancellation of mortgage registrations as not acceptable additionally the court has determined the allocation of costs between the parties the text concludes with the mention of the date and location of the filing

this legal text describes the progress of a case that lasted for 7 years the court ruled in favor of the plaintiff and dismissed the objections raised by the defendants the court ordered the dissolution of the community property and assigned specific properties to each party involved they also ordered the payment of certain sums of money to the parties the court applied legal principles related to the constitution of the italian republic and the civil procedure code they addressed issues of inheritance and the validity of certain deeds the court rejected the defendants objections and upheld the estimates made by the courtappointed technical consultant they dismissed the defendants claims for reimbursement of expenses and ordered fair compensation to the plaintiff for the exclusive occupation of the common property the court also ordered the payment of litigation costs in favor of the plaintiff in this case the court in lecce made several rulings including dismissing preliminary exceptions ordering the dissolution of community property assigning specific properties and adjusting payment amounts they also ordered the transcription of the order by the registrar of real estate records monetary sums were ordered to be paid by certain parties and litigation costs were condemned to be paid by the defendants the court also placed the costs of the courtappointed technical consultant on the parties involved

the court in rome has made a final ruling in a case involving the division of community property between the heirs of two individuals the court has assigned specific real estate properties to each heir based on their respective hereditary shares the court has also ordered the cancellation of mortgages on certain properties and the registration of the division of the properties in the appropriate real estate registers the court has determined the value of each property and the overall value of the divided properties additionally the court has addressed issues related to irregularities in the properties and the establishment of an easement for passage one heir is required to make a cash adjustment payment to the other heir and a mortgage is registered as security for the payment existing mortgages on certain properties have been ordered to be cancelled the court has divided the litigation costs between the parties and determined that the expenses of the courtappointed expert witness will be shared equally the ruling was made on november 10 2018 and filed in the clerks office on december 10 2018

this legal text provides an overview of the progress of a legal case without revealing the names of the parties involved the case begins with one party filing a complaint against the other outlining the issues and seeking a resolution the other party then responds either admitting or denying the allegations both parties exchange information and evidence through a process called discovery to gather facts for their case they may attempt to settle the dispute before trial but if no agreement is reached the case proceeds to trial where arguments and evidence are presented before a judge or jury after the trial a decision is made and if one party is unhappy they can file an appeal the text concludes by stating that the case is ongoing and provides a timeline for the next steps in the legal process the specific case mentioned involves a property dispute between coowners of a house in reggio calabria italy the court has granted the division of the property and the allocation of movable property among the parties the court also discusses legal interpretations regarding the dissolution of inheritance communities related to unauthorized buildings the courtappointed expert confirms irregularities in the propertys construction and determines that it violates urban planning regulations the court rejects the defendants argument to expunge the unauthorized buildings during the division of the property the court orders the defendant to pay litigation costs and assigns different lots to the parties involved the decision was made on march 30 2022 by judge dr gs

in a court case one party was ordered to cover the procedural costs the case involved the division of joint property between two parties the court decided to terminate one partys ownership of immovable property and transfer it to the other party the court also dismissed a claim for additional compensation and ruled on the division of furniture and appliances the court addressed other claims related to loan agreements property rights and lease agreements the case was initiated by one party filing a lawsuit following the dissolution of their marriage the court made its decision based on applicable laws and the circumstances of the case the court determined the division of joint property and the obligations of each party the court also considered the marital property agreement and relevant provisions of family law the court explained that joint property must be determined at the end of a marriage and that the applicable law for dividing joint property is based on the law in force at the time of acquiring the property the court also discussed the progress of a legal case involving apartment ownership and the dispute over the composition of joint property an expert assessment determined the market value of the properties involved the court made decisions regarding compensation for joint property and the division of obligations the court also addressed the ownership of furniture and equipment determining that some items should be considered joint property and others should remain in the sole ownership of one party the court rejected a claim for additional compensation and discussed the division of property rights and obligations arising from a lease agreement the court considered evidence presented by both parties and made its judgment based on the circumstances of the case the court also determined the distribution of procedural costs in the case

this legal text summarizes a court case in ljubljana slovenia the case involves a plaintiff and a defendant and the judgment was made on november 23 2017 the plaintiff incurred costs of 255192 eur which the defendant is responsible for paying the case lasted for 1 year and 3 months from august 24 2016 to november 23 2017 the final resolution of the case involves the division of a real estate property worth 2718420 eur between the two expartners the plaintiff and defendant are entitled to half of the property each the specific sections of the marriage family relations act used in the decision are sections 51 and 59 the court ruled that the coowned property is an apartment and the shares are divided equally between the plaintiff and the defendant the defendant is ordered to register the plaintiffs right to coownership within 15 days the plaintiffs alternative claim is dismissed the defendant is also ordered to pay the plaintiff a specific amount of money with interest the plaintiff withdrew a claim for a certain amount of money and the proceedings regarding that claim are stayed the plaintiff argued that the jointly owned apartment should be considered joint property due to their contributions and the circumstances surrounding their cohabitation the court agreed and determined that the apartment is coowned property however the court rejected the plaintiffs request to have the defendant registered as the sole owner the court discussed the costs of the case and ruled that each party should bear their own costs the text also explains the process of appealing a judgment including the time limit requirements and consequences for noncompliance the text is dated november 23 2017 in ljubljana

this legal case involves multiple parties including the republic of estonia the european union eu vl division common property and ü aa the eu filed a lawsuit against vl division common property requesting a change in the owners entry in the land register vl partially satisfied the eus counterclaim which also involved a change in the owners entry in the land register ü aa filed a lawsuit against the eu and vl claiming 54000 euros in arrears the court ruled that the procedural costs would be covered by one party but the exact amount was not specified the court also made decisions regarding the division of joint property including the sale of a real estate property and an apartment the court rejected the defendants claim to divide a car acquired during the marriage the court stated that the procedural costs would be borne by the parties involved the court also mentioned the possibility of appealing the decision within 30 days procedural aid may be granted to cover the costs of the proceedings if certain requirements are met the divorce division of joint property proceedings began in november 2019 and is still ongoing the court has made various decisions and judgments throughout the case considering the applicable laws and evidence presented by the parties the court has also addressed the validity of a partnership agreement and the contributions made by the partners the court has determined the division of assets and the compensation owed to the parties the court has also resolved disputes over the ownership of a vehicle the court has ordered the parties to bear their own procedural costs and the exact amount will be determined separately

in this legal case the viru county court issued a court order to terminate the proceedings between the claimant ap and the defendant rp the court confirmed a compromise reached by the parties which involved the division of their joint assets acquired during their marriage the defendant will retain sole ownership of one property while the claimant gives consent for the defendant to become the sole owner of another property the defendant also agreed to pay the claimant a total of 13500 euros in monthly installments the claimant waived any further claims against the defendant the parties real estate department of the tartu county court any appeals must be filed within three days of receiving the court order the legal case was initiated on february 27 2023 when the claimant filed a ended in november 2021 and that the defendant has already paid the claimant a sum of money as compensation the parties have now reached a compromise which includes the division of joint assets and payment of a sum of money to the claimant the court approves the compromise and terminates the civil case the parties are informed about their rights to file a new lawsuit or enforce the court order the court order also mentions the refund of the state fee paid by the parties the court will determine the distribution of procedural costs between the parties involved in the case

this legal text summarizes a court case in ragusa italy where a request for personal separation between two spouses was granted the court awarded exclusive custody of the couples minor daughter to the mother and assigned the marital home to her the respondent was ordered to pay a monthly sum of €40000 for the daughters maintenance as well as 50 of any extraordinary expenses additionally the respondent was ordered to pay a compensatory amount of €1000000 along with legal interest and the costs of the litigation the court found that the appellants claim for damages was valid due to the respondents violation of marital duties and awarded compensation for physical and moral damages the court made the final decision in a case registered in 2013 disregarding the objections and separating the spouses the decision was made on october 28 2017 and filed in the clerks office on november 15 2017

this legal text summarizes a court case involving two former spouses mr mo ig and ms ti br the court has ordered the dissolution of their community property and the division of their assets located in campi bisenzio an expert appointed by the court ar pa has determined the allocation of the assets based on their value ms ti br has been assigned a flat while mr mo ig has been assigned a flat and a garage the court has also ordered the payment of certain sums of money between the parties the division of the property was based on a settlement agreement signed by both parties additionally the right of habitation initially held by ms ti br has been assigned to mr mo ig both parties have jointly filed an appeal regarding the property conditions of their divorce the court ruling was made on february 17 2017 in florence

this legal text summarizes a court case that lasted for 11 years the court ruled in favor of dr xxxx declaring the dissolution of a community of properties located in latina borgo carso via pericle the court assigned the properties which include an apartment on the second floor and an uncovered parking space on the ground floor to dr xxxx the defendant was ordered to pay dr xxx a sum of 2252500 euros plus interest the court allocated the litigation costs to be paid by the community with each party responsible for their share the court considered evidence from both parties and an experts report it determined that the properties were not easily divisible and decided to assign them to dr roberto galasso the court also took into account the defendants economic conditions and ordered compensation for exclusive use of the property the court ruled on the case in latina italy rejecting proposed applications and exceptions it identified the properties involved and ordered the dissolution of the community assigning the properties to the heirs of a certain individual the heirs were also ordered to pay a sum of 2252500 euros to the defendant the court allocated the litigation costs to the community and determined the amount to be paid by each coowner the ruling was made on august 6 2020 and filed in the clerks office on july 8 2020

in this legal case a court in palermo has declared the open succession of a deceased individual and identified the assets that make up their estate the estate includes various properties and land located in palermo the court has determined the shares of the estate that each heir is entitled to receive additionally the court has dissolved the existing community property and assigned exclusive ownership of certain assets to one of the parties involved the court has ordered this party to pay adjustment amounts to the other parties based on their shares of the estate the court has also addressed preliminary issues raised by the parties such as the admissibility of certain claims and the simulation of a sale it has been determined that the estate is indivisible and therefore the court has ordered the division of the assets among the heirs the court has also addressed the payment of occupation indemnity and civil fruits enjoyed by one of the parties finally the court has concluded by addressing the costs of the litigation and determining that they should be offset among the parties this ruling was made on october 1 2019

this legal text summarizes the progress of a court case in ljubljana regarding the division of assets between two exspouses the case started on july 9 2018 and ended on december 4 2021 lasting for a total of 3 years 4 months and 25 days the total value of the assets involved is estimated to be 100000 eur including real estate properties a garage business premises and funds in a bank account the division of these assets was determined as 45 for the plaintiff and 55 for the defendant the court considered the principles of the marriage family relations act and certain sections of the civil procedure code in making its decision the court ordered the defendant to provide documents authorizing the registration of the plaintiffs share in the immovable property garage and land the defendant was also required to pay the plaintiff a sum of eur 471354 each party is responsible for bearing their own costs the court considered the income and contributions of each spouse in determining the division of the coowned property the court partially upheld the applicants claim and ordered the division of the property and the registration of their share the court also made a judgment regarding the payment of money the court allowed the addressing of property issues during ongoing litigation under specific circumstances the court determined the shares of the coowned property to be 45 and 55 for the parties involved the court considered the costs of the proceedings based on the civil procedure act the judgment was mostly in favor of one party but had some unresolved aspects the resolution of the dispute was seen as fair and took into account the interests of both parties the text also provides instructions for lodging an appeal including the time limit required information and payment of court fees the document was dated december 14 2021 in ljubljana

in this court case two parties were involved in a dispute over the dissolution of a shared property the property in question was a house in canolo and a piece of land in reggio calabria the court deemed the request for dissolution inadmissible but ordered the division of the property the plaintiff was given exclusive ownership of the house in canolo while the defendant was awarded a sum of eur 14000 the court referred to relevant articles of the civil code and inheritance division rules the decision was based on evidence including an experts report on the properties the court found that the building on the land in pe was not properly permitted and needed to be demolished however the house in canolo met the requirements and could be divided due to the costs and uncertainties of selling the property at auction the court decided to assign the house exclusively to one owner the value of the property was determined by an experts report and the plaintiff was ordered to pay the defendant a balance amount the costs of the proceedings were to be shared equally between the parties the court in reggio calabria made a final ruling on october 26 2021 declaring the application for dissolution improper and ordering the dissolution of the community property in canolo nu the applicant was granted exclusive ownership of a property in canolo and was also required to pay the defendant eur 14000 the costs and fees of the proceedings would be offset between the parties and they were also responsible for sharing the costs of the courtappointed experts report

the livorno court has issued a final ruling in a succession case they have declared the deceased persons succession and determined the entitlements of the parties involved the court has ordered the division of various properties including a marital residence and commercial properties among the parties one party has been ordered to pay a balance to the other party the court has also determined that each party should bear their own litigation costs the judgment was based on relevant provisions of the civil code and the civil procedure code the court rejected objections raised by one party and proceeded with the division of the estate they considered an expert report on the properties and made necessary allocations the court also addressed arguments regarding the right of habitation and use of the properties the surviving spouse has been assigned a larger share of the inheritance and the properties have been assigned accordingly the court has also ordered the reimbursement of expenses incurred in the division process

a court in milan has issued a final resolution to dissolve the community property between two parties referred to as pa and sa al the court assigned specific properties to each party and ordered a cash settlement to balance the division the court considered the involvement of a mortgage creditor and appointed an expert witness to assess the value of the properties based on the experts report the court determined the assignment of properties and the cash settlement the court rejected claims made by both parties regarding alleged debts and expenses the costs of the proceedings were divided equally between the parties the court has dissolved the community property and assigned specific properties to each party the court also ordered a mortgage to be transferred to a bank in favor of one of the parties the costs of the proceedings have been determined and both parties are responsible for paying them the court has also declared that the banks costs are not recoverable

this legal text summarizes the progress of a specific case identified as case n° 42012021 without mentioning the names of the parties involved the case involves two parties one referred to as principle soccumbency who has been ordered to pay a certain amount of money initially they were ordered to pay €3777 but in the second degree the amount increased to €5615 the case lasted for seven years from september 20 2014 to june 25 2021 it revolves around the purchase of a property during a marriage under the legal community regime the nonpurchasing spouse claimed that the property was acquired using personal funds to exclude it from community property the case was appealed and an expert witness was appointed to determine the divisibility and value of the property the appellant argued that the lower courts judgment was erroneous and violated procedural and substantive rules the court ruled in favor of the appellant declaring the property as their exclusive ownership and rejecting the counterclaim for division made by the other party the court also ordered the other party to reimburse the appellant for the costs of the proceedings

in this legal case the court of reggio calabria has made a final ruling regarding a shared property located in bagnara calabra the court has declared the property indivisible and assigned exclusive ownership to one party as a result the other party has been ordered to pay a sum of €25740 the court has also granted the plaintiffs request for payment of the propertys profits awarding them €10800 any credits or debts related to the case have been offset resulting in an obligation for one party to pay €14940 to the other the court has rejected the plaintiffs additional claims and has ordered them to pay the litigation costs of €3230 plus general expenses the court has stated that the applicable law in this case is article 720 of the civil code the ruling was made on september 30 2020

ARTICLE 159 STATUTORY MATRIMONIAL PROPERTY REGIME The statutory matrimonial property regime (in the absence of a different agreement concluded pursuant to Article 162, according to which marital agreements must be concluded by authentic instrument under penalty of nullity, the choice of the separation regime may also be declared in the marriage ceremony, the agreements may be concluded at any time) shall be community of property governed by artt. 177 ff.

ARTICLE 210 CONVENTIONAL CHANGES TO LEGAL COMMUNITY OF PROPERTY The spouses may, by means of an agreement concluded in accordance with Article 162, i.e. by public instrument or by declaration in the marriage certificate, modify the rules governing community of property, provided that the agreements are not contrary to the provisions of Article 161, i.e. the spouses can not stipulate in a general manner that their property relationships shall be governed in whole or in part by laws to which they are not subject or by custom. The property referred to in points (c), (d) and (e) of Article 179, i.e. property for the strictly personal use of each spouse and its accessories, property used in the exercise of the spouse's profession, except for property used to run a business forming part of the community, and property obtained by way of compensation for damage as well as the pension relating to the partial or total loss of the capacity to work cannot be included in the contractual community. The rules of legal community of property regarding the administration of community property and the equality of shares limited to the property that would be the subject of legal community of property cannot be derogated from.

85 MARITAL PROPERTY AGREEMENT (1) A marital property agreement is a contract whereby the spouses determine their property regime that differs from the legal property regime. The agreement may, by their common accord, regulate other property affairs during the marriage and also in the case of divorce. (2) The agreed property regime shall apply to spouses as of the date of conclusion of the agreement, unless the parties agree otherwise. (3) A marital property agreement concluded by future spouses shall take effect on the day of conclusion of marriage or on a later day determined by the spouses in the marital property agreement. (4) The marital property agreement shall be entered in the register of marital property agreements. (5) Unless otherwise provided by this Code, the provisions of this Code on the conclusion of marital property agreements between spouses (post-nuptial contracts) shall apply to the conclusion of marital property agreements between future spouses (pre-nuptial contracts). (6) Before concluding a marital property agreement the spouses shall inform the other spouse of their material standing; otherwise the marital property agreement shall be challengeable. (7) The provisions of this Code on conclusion of marital property contracts between spouses shall apply, mutatis mutandis, to the conclusion of marital property contracts between cohabiting partners. FAMILY
92 ESTABLISHING A REGISTER The register of marital property agreements shall be established by the Chamber of Notaries of Slovenia. FAMILY

28. Principles of joint administration of joint property (1) Unless otherwise agreed, spouses shall jointly exercise the rights and perform the obligations relating to joint property. Spouses have the right to possess jointly the objects forming part of their joint property. (2) Spouses may transfer the right to administer joint property to one of the spouses by a marital property contract. (3) Spouses shall administer joint property in the interests of matrimonial cohabitation and regular management of the property. They shall inform each other of the acts of administration of property and of the financial status. (4) A spouse may require conclusion of transactions necessary for correction of an entry concerning an object included in the joint property to be entered in the register.

ARTICLE 167 CONSTITUTION OF A MATRIMONIAL PROPERTY FUND Either or both spouses, by public deed, or a third party, including by will, may set up a property fund, allocating certain assets, whether real or movable property, entered in public registers or securities, to meet the family's needs. The constitution of the matrimonial property fund by deed between living persons, carried out by the third party, is perfected by the acceptance of the spouses. Acceptance may be made by a subsequent public deed. The constitution may also be made during marriage. Debt securities must be pledged by making them registered with a note of the pledge or in another adequate manner.

61. Legal effect of marital property contract with regard to third persons (1) Any changes in a proprietary relationship made on the basis of a marital property contract shall be entered in the marital property register. (2) If spouses change a proprietary relationship applied with respect to them pursuant to § 24 of this Act or terminate a proprietary relationship, the changes shall have legal effect with regard to a third person only if the changes have been entered in the marital property register as a marital property contract or the third person was aware of the existence of the marital property contract. (3) Subsections 1 and 2 of this section apply also if the spouses terminate or amend a marital property contract or an order concerning proprietary relations entered in the marital property register on the basis of a court judgment. (4) The marital property register shall be maintained pursuant to the procedure provided for in the Marital Property Register Act.

ARTICLE 180 ADMINISTRATION OF COMMUNITY PROPERTY The administration of the community property and the representation in court for acts relating thereto belong separately to both spouses. The performance of acts beyond ordinary administration, as well as the conclusion of contracts granting or acquiring personal rights of enjoyment and the representation in court for related actions are jointly entitled to both spouses.

ARTICLE 211 OBLIGATIONS OF SPOUSES CONTRACTED BEFORE MARRIAGE The community property are liable for obligations contracted by one of the spouses before the marriage limited to the value of the property owned by that spouse before the marriage which, by agreement entered into, became part of the community of property.

24. Selection of proprietary relationship Prospective spouses may, by agreement, select a proprietary relationship from among the types of proprietary relations set out in Subchapter 2 of this Chapter before the contraction of marriage by an application for marriage pursuant to the procedure prescribed in the Vital Statistics Registration Act. The abovementioned declaration of intention takes effect upon the contraction of marriage. If the prospective spouses do not select a proprietary relationship by an application for marriage and do not enter into a marital property contract, the provisions regarding jointness of property shall apply to their proprietary relations as of the contraction of marriage. The provisions regarding jointness of property shall also apply if the spouses have not selected the type of proprietary relations by an agreement to apply Estonian law to the proprietary rights. If spouses select a proprietary relationship by an application for marriage and, in addition, enter into a marital property contract, the marital property contract shall be applied to the proprietary relations between the spouses.
63 PROPERTY REGIME Property regime shall mean all the rules governing property relationships between spouses and in relation to third parties. FAMILY

211. Marital property right (1) In the case of marriages contracted before entry into force of this Act, the provisions of this Act regarding jointness of property apply to the proprietary relations between spouses as of entry into force of this Act unless otherwise provided for in a marital property contract entered into before entry into force of this Act. (2) In the proprietary relationship of jointness of property the procedure for administration of separate property provided for in § 271 of this Act applies to the property acquired after entry into force of § 271

90 REGISTER OF MARITAL PROPERTY AGREEMENTS (1) The register of marital property agreements is an electronic database maintained by the Chamber of Notaries of Slovenia. (2) When future spouses who have signed a marital property agreement marry, they shall send the extract from the register of births, marriages and deaths (civil register) containing data on the marriage to the notary that drew up the marital property agreement. (3) A notary who draws up a marital property agreement or an amendment to a marital property agreement shall send to the Chamber of Notaries of Slovenia a request to enter such agreement in the register of marital property agreements within eight days of the date of receipt of the extract from the civil register containing data on marriage or within eight days from the conclusion or amendment of the marital property agreement concluded or amended after the conclusion of marriage. Where the spouses conclude or amend a marital property agreement which does not come into effect on the day of conclusion of marriage or on the day of conclusion or amendment of the marital property agreement, the notary shall send to the Chamber of Notaries of Slovenia a request to enter such agreement in the register of marital property agreements within eight days of the date of effect of the marital property agreement. (4) Where spouses conclude marriage abroad, they shall send to the notary the extract from the civil register containing data on the marriage, unless otherwise provided by an international treaty. The notary shall send to the Chamber of Notaries of Slovenia a request for entry of the agreement in the register of marital property agreements within eight days of the date of receipt of the extract. (5) A notary who draws up a marital property agreement or an amendment to a marital property agreement shall indicate in the request for entry in the register of marital property agreements the data referred to in paragraph two of Article 93 of this Code that are available to them. (6) A request for entry of termination of validity of a marital property agreement shall be sent to the notary that drew up the agreement of the spouses who had concluded the marital property agreement or by their legal successors. The notary shall send to the Chamber of Notaries of Slovenia a request for entry of termination of marital property agreement complete with termination date, within eight days of the date of receipt of the request. FAMILY

this legal text summarizes a court case that took place in como italy in 2006 the case involved two parties referred to as re and rfrfi who coowned a piece of real estate re sued rfrfi requesting the dissolution of the coownership and division of the property re wanted the property to be divided through an auction while rfrfi wanted to keep their respective shares the courtappointed expert determined the value of the property and the court granted res request for dissolution the property will be divided through drawing lots the court rejected rfrfis counterclaim for compensation and deemed it inadmissible the court explained the legal principles and criteria for property division and concluded that drawing lots was the most appropriate method the court also addressed legal interest and monetary revaluation the courts decision will be implemented through a separate order the document is dated february 1 2006 and signed by the judge president and another individual

ARTICLE 168 USE AND ADMINISTRATION OF THE FUND Ownership of the property constituting the matrimonial property fund belongs to both spouses, unless otherwise stipulated in the deed of constitution. The fruits of the assets constituting the patrimonial fund are used for the needs of the family. The administration of the property constituting the matrimonial property fund is governed by the rules on the administration of legal community.

65 PRESUMPTION OF LEGAL MARITAL PROPERTY REGIME A legal marital property regime shall apply to spouses, unless they agree on the contents of the property regime through an agreement on property relationships. In such case, the agreed property regime shall apply to them. FAMILY

66 LEGAL MARITAL PROPERTY REGIME A legal marital property regime between spouses shall be a community property regime for the co-owned property of spouses and a separate property regime for the personal property of each spouse. FAMILY

ARTICLE 194 DIVISION OF COMMUNITY PROPERTY The division of community property is performed by dividing the assets and liabilities equally. The court, in relation to the needs of the children and their custody, may constitute in favour of one of the spouses the usufruct on a part of the property due to the other spouse.

ARTICLE 185 ADMINISTRATION OF A SPOUSE'S PERSONAL PROPERTY The provisions of the second, third and fourth paragraphs of Article 217 apply to the administration of property not forming part of a community of property or an estate [167-171 of the Civil Code].

content: In Estonia, if you are entitled to a compulsory portion (or reserved portion) of an inheritance, it amounts to half of what you would have received if the deceased had died intestate (without a will), assuming all intestate heirs accept the inheritance (Article 105). If you have been left a smaller share in a will than your compulsory portion, you can claim the difference from the other heirs. Similarly, if you receive a legacy that is less than your compulsory portion, you may also claim the difference, provided you renounce the legacy (Article 105). The specific amount you receive will depend on the total value of the estate and the number of heirs involved. The details of your compulsory portion will be documented in a certificate authenticated by a notary, which will outline the recipient and the size of the compulsory portion as a legal share of the estate (Article 173). For precise calculations or further legal advice, it is recommended to consult a legal professional.

ACT 105. Size of compulsory portion A compulsory portion is one-half of the value of the share of an estate which a successor would have received in the case of intestate succession if all intestate successors would have accepted the succession. The persons who renounce a succession by a contract shall not be taken into account upon determining the size of a compulsory portion. If a person entitled to claim a compulsory portion has been bequeathed a share of an estate which is smaller than a compulsory portion, he or she may claim the deficient share as a compulsory portion from the co-successors. If one from several successors has the right to receive a compulsory portion, he or she may refuse to satisfy the claim of some other person entitled to compulsory portion to such extent that he or she would receive his or her compulsory portion. Other successors shall be liable for the deficit. If a legacy is given to a person entitled to a compulsory portion, he or she has a claim for the compulsory portion only in case he or she renounces the legacy. If the value of a legacy is lower than a compulsory portion, the person may claim a compulsory portion from the successors to the extent which he or she received less as legacy than he or she would have received as a compulsory portion.

ACT 173. Certificate of recipient of compulsory portion A notary shall authenticate a certificate concerning the claim arising from a compulsory portion (hereinafter certificate of recipient of compulsory portion) on the basis of a notarially authenticated application of the successor, the executor of the will or the person entitled to a compulsory portion. The certificate of recipient of compulsory portion shall set out the recipient and the size of the compulsory portion as a legal share of the estate.
